titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Kent Tenney Healy Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by John Espy

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 28, 2022 at 08:45

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, teaching materials, minutes, reports, and memoranda relating to various Yale University committees and departments with which Kent Healy was connected. As a specialist in transportation he was a member of a number of government committees, among them the National Resources Planning Board, the New England Council Transportation Committee, and the New Haven Traffic Commission. His subject files contain extensive printed and mimeographed material on railroads.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The majority of the materials are open for research. As established by Yale Corporation regulations., student records in box 22 are restricted until 2041; grade books in box 24 are restricted until 2024.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kent Tenney Healy Papers (MS 653). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Historical Picture Collection (MS 713), Series III, A 148, 1-11, for eleven undated lantern slides re: Italian railroads and highways which were removed from the Kent T. Healy Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kent T. Healy Papers cover the period of time that Kent T. Healy was a member of the Committee on Transportation of the Economics department at Yale. The first five boxes of the papers contain Professor Healy's correspondence mostly with his colleagues in the academic and business worlds. Most of the correspondence falls within the time span of the 1930's and 1940's. Also included in the collection are typed manuscripts and notes for Professor Healy's papers on various aspects of transportation, as well as student papers. Professor Healy's main transportation concern was with railroads, and boxes 12 and 13 contain information files on various national and foreign railroads. Boxes 15-17 contain folders from the Committee on Transportation, and the rest of the collection is taken up with reports from various organizations, mimeographed papers of Professor Healy's, and printed material such as books, pamphlets, and newspaper clippings.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kent T. Healy was born in Chicago on February 2, 1902. He received his B.A. from Harvard in 1921, and his B.S. in Electrical Engineering from M.I.T. in 1923. Healey was an assistant professor of transportation at Yale from 1928-1937, an assistant professor of political economy from 1937-1938, an assistant professor of economics from 1938-1940, an associate professor from 1940-1945, and the Thomas DeWitt Cuyler Professor of Transportation from 1945-1970.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Yale departments and committees: correspondence, memoranda, reports
Correspondence re: Thomas DeWitt Cuyler Chair of Transportation
Executive Committee of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences
Executive Committee of the Social Sciences
Tenure Appointments Committee in the Sciences
Transportation Council for the Department of Commerce
New York State office of Transportation
Transportation, especially of coal and oil; Bituminous Coal Division, Dept. of the Interior (BCD); and Lend -Lease Administration: correspondence, MSS, notes
Transportation, mostly railroads: MSS, notes, some student papers
Transportation, mostly railroads: MSS, notes, some student papers
Mostly railroads and BCD: reports, printed matter
Mostly railroads and BCD: reports, printed matter
Mostly railroads and BCD: reports, printed matter
Mostly railroads and BCD: reports, printed matter
Mostly railroads: mimeographed material (including course hand-outs), printed matter, some MSS and correspondence
Mostly railroads: mimeographed material (including course hand-outs), printed matter, some MSS and correspondence
Mostly railroads: mimeographed material (including course hand-outs), printed matter, some MSS and correspondence
Papers concerning the application of the Chicago, Burlington & Quincy Railroad Company before the Interstate Commerce Commission: correspondence, MSS, printed matter
Letters of recommendation, correspondence with students, papers concerning fellowships

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Student files are restricted until January 1, 2041 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Grade books are closed until January 1, 2024 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: New England Steamship Company: notes and charts
Miscellaneous Yale papers (oversize), mostly concerning Committee on Transportation
